outbound_official.notes: Official Hungarian MFA Consular Information page listing countries where Hungarian citizens (ordinary private passport holders, M) may travel without a prior consular visa. Entries marked with D/SZ only (diplomatic/service passport required) are excluded from the visa_free list as they do not cover ordinary passport holders. Some entries have pre-travel online registration requirements (eTA / digital arrival card) but no prior consular visa. The page explicitly states this list is informational; travelers should verify current conditions with the destination country embassy before travel. Page accessed June 2026 via direct HTTP fetch from konzinfo.mfa.gov.hu (TLS chain issue on client).

visa_free.name: Algeria
visa_free.notes: Diplomatic/service passport only (30/90 days); ordinary passport NOT visa-free

visa_free.name: Belarus
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport: 30 days (special conditions apply — see travel advice); diplomatic/service: 90 days

visa_free.name: China
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport: 30 days (business, tourism, family visit, transit, networking only); diplomatic: 90 days; service: 30 days

visa_free.name: Croatia
visa_free.notes: 90 days; EU free movement; all current SZIG types accepted

visa_free.name: Kyrgyzstan
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport: 30 days (from 1 January 2026; re-entry allowed after 30-day gap); diplomatic/service: 30 days

visa_free.name: Mexico
visa_free.notes: 180 days; tourism and business only for ordinary passport

visa_free.name: Mongolia
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport: 30 days (verify with Mongolian embassy before travel); diplomatic/service: 90 days

visa_free.name: Montenegro
visa_free.notes: Ordinary passport + D + SZ: 90 days; SZIG: 30 days

visa_free.name: Peru
visa_free.notes: 90 days (immigration officer may grant fewer days; supporting documents recommended)

visa_free.name: Turkey
visa_free.notes: 90 days; SZIG card accepted for tourism/transit only

visa_free.name: Zambia
visa_free.notes: 90 days (tourism/private visit within 1 year); stays over 30 days require extension at immigration

visa_on_arrival.name: Oman
visa_on_arrival.notes: Ordinary passport: max 14 days tourism, visa issued on arrival; diplomatic/service: 90 days visa-free. Conditions: tourism purpose, return ticket, accommodation, insurance, passport valid 6+ months after departure

eta.name: United States
eta.notes: ESTA (Electronic System for Travel Authorization) required before boarding; valid for tourism/business up to 90 days. Biometric passport required.

eta.name: Canada
eta.notes: eTA (Electronic Travel Authorization) required for air travel and transit; up to 6 months. Other purposes (work, study) require visa.

eta.name: Kenya
eta.notes: Electronic Travel Authorization (ETA) mandatory before each entry via etakenya.go.ke; 90 days

eta.name: Taiwan
eta.notes: Online registration via Taiwan immigration authority (Online Immigration Arrival Card) required before arrival; 90 days

eta.name: Malaysia
eta.notes: Malaysia Digital Arrival Card (MDAC) online pre-registration required; 90 days. Not a visa — entry remains visa-free.

eta.nationality: All visa-free nationals listed in EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II (except EU/EEA/CH citizens)
eta.notes: ETIAS (European Travel Information and Authorisation System) will be mandatory for all visa-exempt third-country nationals entering Schengen area including Hungary. Confirmed for Q4 2026 launch, with a 6-month transitional period (travellers meeting all other entry conditions will not be refused entry solely for lacking ETIAS during transition, extending into approximately April 2027). ETIAS is valid for 3 years or until passport expires. Fee applies. As of June 2026, ETIAS is not yet operational.

visa_policy.notes: Hungary is a Schengen Area member. Visa-free nationals may stay up to 90 days in any 180-day period. All other third-country nationals require a Schengen visa (C visa) for short stays or a national D visa for long stays. The official list reflects EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II as implemented by Hungary. ETIAS (electronic travel authorization) will be required for visa-free visitors once operational (planned Q4 2026, with transition period into April 2027). Certain nationalities with bilateral agreements may stay an additional 30 or 90 days in Hungary beyond the standard Schengen allowance, subject to conditions (cannot travel to other Schengen states, must have not used full 90-day Schengen allocation in prior 6 months, must depart from Hungary).

conditional_access.conditions: Per EU Regulation No 265/2010 of 25 March 2010: a third-country national holding a valid long-stay visa (Type D) issued by any Schengen Member State may travel to Hungary and other Schengen states for up to 90 days in any 180-day period, under the same conditions as a residence permit holder. Must hold a valid travel document and meet other entry conditions.

cbi.notes: Hungary does not have a citizenship-by-investment program. The Guest Investor Program provides a residence permit only (valid up to 10 years, renewable for additional 10-year periods), not a pathway to citizenship. Guest investors are explicitly excluded from obtaining a national residence card (permanent residence). Hungary does allow dual citizenship under its citizenship law; applicants for naturalization do not need to renounce prior nationality.

programs.name: Guest Investor Residence Permit
programs.notes: Two investment options: (1) Acquisition of investment fund share of at least EUR 250,000 issued by a real estate fund registered by the Hungarian National Bank — fund must allocate at least 40% of net asset value to Hungarian residential real estate; investor must hold for minimum 5 years. (2) Financial donation of at least EUR 1,000,000 to a public-trust higher education institution for educational, scientific research or artistic activities. A direct residential property purchase pathway (EUR 500,000) was abolished as of 15 January 2025. Permit valid up to 10 years, renewable for additional 10-year periods (no longer limited to one renewal). Guest investors explicitly CANNOT obtain a national residence card (permanent residence). No path to citizenship via this program. Work permitted without restrictions. Processing time: 21 days standard.

programs.name: EU Blue Card (Hungary)
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with tertiary education or 3+ years relevant professional experience (within past 7 years for ICT/executive roles) working in a position requiring high-level qualifications. Minimum salary: HUF 883,671/month (2025); HUF 1,001,048/month (2026). Healthcare roles: HUF 706,937/month (2025); HUF 800,838/month (2026).
programs.processing_time: 70 days standard; 30 days for existing EU Blue Card holders from other EU member states
programs.notes: Initial validity up to 4 years, renewable for up to 4 years at a time. Employment restrictions apply during first year. 2026 salary thresholds apply retroactively to 2025-initiated applications with 2026 decisions. Healthcare 2026 threshold updated to HUF 800,838.

programs.name: White Card – Residency for Digital Nomads
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals with verified employment relationship or company ownership outside Hungary, working remotely from Hungary using digital technology. Must not pursue any gainful activity in Hungary or hold shares in Hungarian companies. Minimum net monthly income: EUR 3,000 for at least 6 months prior to entry.
programs.notes: Initial validity: maximum 1 year. Extendable once for additional 1 year (total maximum 2 years). White Card holders CANNOT obtain a national residence card (permanent residence). Family reunification is not available for White Card holders (except for children born during the permit's validity). Must have resided in Hungary for at least 90 days within any 180-day period before extension application.

programs.name: Guest Self-Employment Residence Permit
programs.eligibility: Third-country nationals pursuing self-employed activities or serving as chief executive of a business organization. Self-employed must demonstrate annual income exceeding 24 times the current minimum wage (via tax authority certification). Business executives: organization must employ at least 5 Hungarian/EEA nationals full-time for 6 consecutive months, or demonstrate sufficient income.
programs.notes: Initial permit: maximum 1 year. Extendable up to 2 additional years (total maximum 3 years from first issuance). Holders CANNOT obtain a national residence card (permanent residence). Family reunification restricted for first year after issuance.

validation_notes: CORRECTION (2026-06-14): Blanket suspension of ALL bilateral diplomatic/service/official passport waivers until 6 March 2027 propagated to all 40 conditional_access passport_type entries. Prior record noted this suspension only for Georgia; official Hungarian consular source (konzuliszolgalat.kormany.hu) confirms it applies universally.

CORRECTION (2026-06-14): ETIAS timeline updated — confirmed Q4 2026 launch with 6-month transition period extending into approximately April 2027. Prior record stated 'planned Q4 2026' without noting the transition period.
CORRECTION (2026-06-14): EU Blue Card 2026 healthcare salary threshold added: HUF 800,838/month (was absent from prior record; confirmed from oif.gov.hu/factsheets/eu-blue-card).

CORRECTION (2026-06-14): RBI Guest Investor program notes updated — permit renewals confirmed as renewable for additional 10-year periods (not limited to one renewal as previously implied), per oif.gov.hu factsheet updated March 3, 2026.

ADDITION (2026-06-14): Bilateral ordinary-passport extended stay details added to visa_free notes for 20 countries (Andorra, Argentina, Brazil, Canada, Chile, Costa Rica, Hong Kong SAR, Israel, Japan, Macao SAR, Malaysia, Mexico, New Zealand, Panama, Paraguay, San Marino (30 days), Singapore (30 days), South Korea, USA, Uruguay, Venezuela) — confirmed from oif.gov.hu/factsheets/information-notice-for-persons-entitled-to-a-visa-free-stay.
CORRECTION (2026-06-14): White Card family reunification note corrected — children born during the permit's validity are an exception to the no-family-reunification rule, per oif.gov.hu factsheet.

VERIFIED (2026-06-14): Ordinary-passport visa-free list confirmed accurate against oif.gov.hu/factsheets/visa-free-citizens (68 states + Hong Kong SAR + Macao SAR + British special categories + Taiwan + Kosovo).
VERIFIED (2026-06-14): CBI: no program confirmed. Guest Investor program provides residence only.
VERIFIED (2026-06-14): Real estate direct purchase pathway (EUR 500,000) confirmed abolished 15 January 2025.

ADDITION (2026-06-14): outbound_official field added — Hungarian MFA official page 'Vízummentes külföldre utazás' (https://konzinfo.mfa.gov.hu/vizummentes-kulfoldre-utazas) confirmed as the origin-side authoritative list for where Hungarian ordinary passport holders can travel without a prior consular visa. Page content retrieved via direct HTTP fetch. Full country table extracted and structured into visa_free, visa_on_arrival, and eta categories.
gaps: Exact scope of which passport types are covered by the blanket diplomatic/service/official suspension (until 6 March 2027): the official search result states 'diplomatic, service and official passports' but some bilateral agreements in the record include 'special' passports — unclear whether 'special' passports are covered by the suspension.
Exact naturalization residence requirement years for standard (non-simplified) naturalization pathway could not be confirmed from official English-language sources.

Biometric passport conditions for some Annex II countries (e.g., Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Georgia, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia, Ukraine) noted in some sources but not explicitly stated in the official oif.gov.hu factsheet retrieved.
ETIAS fee amount not confirmed from official source.
Taiwan passport condition (must contain identity card number) noted in secondary sources but not extracted directly from official page.
Macao SAR biometric/specific passport type condition not explicitly verified from official English text.
OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) card: no official Hungarian or Indian government source found granting OCI cardholders any special entry privileges to Hungary. Not recorded.
Bilateral agreement dates/names: the official MFA table lists diplomatic/service/official passport waivers but does not provide agreement names or signing dates.

Full list of countries covered by blanket diplomatic/service/official passport suspension not independently extracted from the official table (konzuliszolgalat.kormany.hu had SSL error); suspension confirmed via search results referencing this page.

Whether Georgia's ordinary-passport visa-free status (Annex II) is affected by the diplomatic/service/official passport suspension — it is not; ordinary passport visa-free access for Georgia is under EU Regulation 2018/1806 Annex II and is unaffected.

outbound_official: Page date/last-updated timestamp not visible in fetched HTML (no 'asof' value captured). Belarus ordinary passport conditions require checking travel advice section for special conditions not detailed in the table. Oman ordinary passport described as visa-on-arrival (max 14 days) — this may change; travelers should verify.

outbound_official: Algeria, Bahrain, Cambodia, Cuba, Egypt, Ghana, India, Indonesia, Iran, Jordan, Kuwait, Laos, Lebanon, Maldives, Pakistan, Qatar, Russia, Turkmenistan, Yemen — these appear in the official outbound table only for diplomatic/service passport holders (not ordinary M passport); they are NOT visa-free for ordinary Hungarian passport holders and are excluded from outbound_official.visa_free.
